descriptionParagraphs:
For groms who want to go fast! The MF Bolt is a lively 'all-rounder' that lets younger surfers go hard while being super easy to surf. It's basically a scaled-down version of Mick Fanning's popular 'Lightning' model.

Your versatile performance board designed for everyday conditions.
Fin system: FCS II / Futures
Color: Blue, Orange, Grey, Red, Yellow
Glassing: Strong Lite, 4 X 5 Oz
Rocker: Straighter exit rocker for easier speed and handling
Concave: Single concave with a pulled-tail for lively small-wave performance
Rails: Medium to Low Rails
Tail: Narrow Squash
Fins: 3 Fin Thruster (choose Futures or FCS2)

How it surfs: The MF Bolt has plenty of spark while being easy to turn. Super versatile, it generates speed in weaker surf and delivers enough bite to keep your surfing sharp. A user-friendly rocker adds confidence on steeper drops and a refined tail lets you whip things around on any section that pops up.
Quiver: This is your everyday go-to. Your first fibreglass shredder. The MF Bolt fills the gap between entry-level boards and the ultra-high performance Juliette Junior.

The MF Lightning is one of those once-in-a-lifetime boards that earns a permanent place in the rack. Born from Mick Fanning's original CT-winning DNA model, it keeps the same proven foundation but has been fine-tuned for more everyday spark. Mick worked closely with Darren post-retirement to create a faster, sharper version that thrives in smaller, weaker surf, but still holds its own when things get bigger but lack push.
Fin system: FCS II / Futures
Color: Orange, Grey, Red, Blue, Purple, Green, Yellow
Rocker: Same tail rocker as the DNA, but straightened between the feet for more speed. Increased nose lift to fit tight in the pocket.
Concave: Flatter under the front foot for instant acceleration; deep single concave between the fins for extra lift and speed.
Rails: Dropped rails for sensitivity
Tail: Narrower squash tail with no hip for added control; nose slightly narrower with extra flip.

How it surfs: The MF Lightning feels fast, sharp and lively underfoot where it generates instant speed and holds in through turns without washing speed off. It thrives in weaker to medium waves where that extra 'get up and go' makes all the difference, staying reactive and precise in the pocket for crips arcs and tight wraps. Thanks to the narrower tail and refined rails, it maintains control even at high speeds, giving you the confidence to push harder in smaller or softer conditions.
Quiver: The MF Lightning is the board for everyday conditions, it surfs fast and sharp in all conditions ranging from 1-4ft. A great all-round everyday board you can rely on.

Forty years, 100,000+ boards, 11 World Titles, 16 Bells, 2 Stab in the Dark crowns.

Four decades in the bay and DH is still running the planer himself. To mark the milestone he's shaping just forty early-'90s inspired sleds --- built by hand, dialled for today's waves.

Own a piece of surfing history

Pure Handley DNA -- hand-shaped start-to-finish by Darren, signed and sequentially numbered 1 -- 40.

90's shape -- longer outline, old-school rocker and glass-on black fins for that classic drive.

Coloured rails -- a warm vintage resin tint runs nose-to-tail, framing the clean deck and giving each board a one-of-a-kind heirloom look (exact shade will vary slightly---embrace the charm).

Collector-grade construction -- premium PU blank, traditional wet-rub finish, and heritage DHD skull logos straight from the archives.
Color: Orange
Glassing: 6 x 4 x 4 OZ
Rocker: Lots
Concave: Slight Single To Double Concave with Vee on Tail
Rails: Medium Boxy Rails
Tail: Tight Squash Tail
Fins: Thruster. Glassed in Fins

Quiver: If you've ever wanted to hang a board that could just as easily win a heat as it could dominate your living-room wall, this is it. Only forty will leave the factory - when they're gone, they're gone.

The Black Diamond is DHD's true all-rounder - a genuine one-board quiver designed to handle almost anything you're likely to surf, and it's now available in our Soft Top construction!

With extra flotation through the front half for easy paddling and wave entry, it stays forgiving and user-friendly in small, weak surf, while still offering enough performance to handle steeper, better waves when they turn on. It's a board that covers a lot of ground and suits a wide range of surfers and conditions.
Fin system: Futures
Color: Blue, Grey
Glassing: EPS core with 10cm carbon reinforced stringer
Rocker: Medium to low entry rocker for speed and easy acceleration
Concave: Single to double concave flowing into vee out the tail
Rails: Forgiving through the front, more refined through the tai
Tail: Rounded Pin
Fins: Five-fin setup (Thruster or Quad compatible -- FCS II or Futures)

How it surfs: The Black Diamond is easy to paddle, quick to get moving, and forgiving underfoot. The extra width and volume up front help generate speed early and glide through weaker sections, while the pulled-in rounded pin tail and refined rear rails let the board sit comfortably in the pocket when waves get steeper. It feels fast off the front foot, smooth through turns, and predictable at speed. Whether it's one-foot slop or cleaner, more powerful surf, the Black Diamond adapts without feeling out of place.

Quiver: This is your do-everything board --- the one you grab when you don't want to think too hard about conditions. If you want a single board that paddles easily, works across a wide wave range, and still lets you surf properly when the waves improve, the Black Diamond earns its spot.

The Nexus is where innovation meets versatility - a hybrid design combining the rail-to-rail ease of the 3DV with the unmatched speed of the Utopia. Built specifically for beach breaks and wave pools, it's been tested across multiple pools and a variety of small-wave ocean conditions to ensure it performs everywhere.

Loved by our team riders in 1--4ft surf, the Nexus gives you instant acceleration, easy paddle-in, and the ability to glide through flat sections while staying tight and reactive in the pocket. If you want explosive small-wave performance in both the ocean and wave pools, this is the board.
Fin system: Futures / FCS II
Color: Clear
Glassing: 4 x 4 x 3/4 patch 5Oz.
Rocker: Medium Entry, Low Exit Rocker
Concave: Single To Slight Double Concave Between Fins
Rails: Medium Rails
Tail: Slight Narrow Squash Tail
Fins: Thruster, 3 Fin.

How it surfs: The Nexus is fast, playful, and incredibly user-friendly. The Utopia-inspired rocker gives you explosive speed and instant squirt on takeoff - perfect for waves where you only get 3 - 5 seconds to work with. The 3DV's influence makes it roll rail-to-rail with ease, letting you stay tight in the pocket and react quickly in cuppy, short-transition surf. It paddles well thanks to the fuller nose and flat deck, carries momentum through flat spots, and holds plenty of spark off the top.

Quiver: The Nexus is your go-to small-wave performance board. The one you grab for average beachies, summer conditions, and every wave pool session. It sits below your everyday shortboard and above your groveller: more performance than a stubby small-wave board, but quicker and more forgiving than your HP shortboard.

Introducing the Phoenix - Flight -- an evolved take on our best-selling small-wave model, designed to push your surfing further.

The Phoenix - Flight amps up responsiveness through refined design features from the original Phoenix, released in early 2020. With a sleeker, pulled-in tail and a thinner profile, this board enhances maneuverability, allowing for tighter, more controlled turns. The reduced concave under the front foot boosts speed, gliding through flat sections with ease and giving you extra drive in weaker surf.

We've also added more lift and refinement to the nose outline, providing quicker transitions, smoother paddling, and sharper precision when carving. The Phoenix - Flight is built to perform in anything from playful small waves to head-high surf, giving you versatility to thrive in varying conditions.

With a tri-quad fin configuration, you can ride it as a thruster, quad, or even a twin, dialling in the perfect setup for your style and the day's conditions.

If you're looking for a board that's faster, more dynamic, and effortlessly sharp, the Phoenix - Flight is ready to rip into summer conditions!
Fin system: Futures / FCS II
Color: Clear
Glassing: 4 x 4x4
Rocker: Flat
Concave: Shallow Single to Double between fins
Rails: Medium Low Rails
Tail: Squash
Fins: 5 Fin - Tri Quad

How it surfs: The Phoenix - Flight is designed for fast and reactive surfing, making weak surf faster, sharper, and more dynamic. It maintains the original Phoenix's easy paddle-in, instant speed, and playful feel, but with reworked outline, rocker, and concaves for enhanced performance. The sleeker nose, narrowed tail, and thinner profile allow for quicker transitions, tighter turns, and more control in punchier, chest-high sections. It can be ridden as a thruster, quad, or twin, adapting to various conditions and surfing styles.

Quiver: The Phoenix - Flight is the refined evolution of DHD's best-selling small-wave model. It's built to make weak surf faster, sharper, and more dynamic, pushing your surfing further. It's ideal for intermediate to advanced surfers looking for a versatile board that performs well in anything from playful small waves to head-high surf.

The Juliette is Ethan Ewing's update file that he rode to success at his historical win at Bells in 2023 and showed off its moves at Trestles during the finals, securing Ethan a solid world #2 ranking that same year. The Juliette still sits in the DNA family with more nose and tail rocker that helps with tighter turns and puts you in the pocket.
Fin system: Futures / FCS II
Color: Clear Rails Black Logo, Black Rails Orange Logo, Black Rails White Logo, Yellow Rails Orange Logo
Glassing: 4 x 4 x 4 OZ
Rocker: DNA Rocker With Extra Nose & Tail Lift
Concave: Slight Single To Double Concave
Rails: Medium Rails
Tail: Round Tail
Fins: Thruster, 3 Fin *Futures or FCS II

How it surfs: The Juliette is built for speed and commitment. Reducing concave up front gives it instant acceleration --- exactly what Ethan wants when driving off the front foot. The narrowed tail lets you push hard without losing control, and the thinner, narrowed nose allows Ethan's trademark nose-pick rebounds, tight jams, and fast recoveries. The rolled deck and thinner rails make it easy to bury rail at high speed, giving that clean, powerful top-turn line Ethan is known for. It's fast, responsive, and confident when you're pushing into the lip. This board rewards surfers who drive hard, surf tight in the pocket, and want a board that keeps up with rapid direction changes.

Quiver: This is your top-tier high-performance shortboard. When the waves have shape, push, or offer any kind of section to hit, the Juliette is the board you grab. It sits above the DNA in terms of sensitivity and responsiveness --- a more refined, more reactive version built for advanced surfers who want to surf fast, tight, and on rail. Ideal for quality beachies, points, reefs, and any wave where precision matters.

The Juliette is Ethan Ewing's updated high-performance shortboard --- the same file he rode to his historic Bells win in 2023 and the board he put on full display at Trestles on his run to World #2. Still part of the DNA family, the Juliette adds more nose and tail rocker for tighter turns, pocket surfing, and sharper timing at high speed. It's tuned directly around Ethan's style: fast, strong rail engagement, and full-commitment turns.
Fin system: Futures / FCS II
Color: Clear, Fluro Yellow, Black
Glassing: 5 X 5 OZ
Rocker: DNA Rocker With Extra Nose & Tail Lift
Concave: Slight Single To Double Concave
Rails: Medium Rails
Tail: Slight Narrow Squash Tail
Fins: Thruster, 3 Fin *Futures or FCS II

How it surfs: The Juliette is built for speed and commitment. Reducing concave up front gives it instant acceleration --- exactly what Ethan wants when driving off the front foot. The narrowed tail lets you push hard without losing control, and the thinner, narrowed nose allows Ethan's trademark nose-pick rebounds, tight jams, and fast recoveries. The rolled deck and thinner rails make it easy to bury rail at high speed, giving that clean, powerful top-turn line Ethan is known for. It's fast, responsive, and confident when you're pushing into the lip. This board rewards surfers who drive hard, surf tight in the pocket, and want a board that keeps up with rapid direction changes.

Quiver: This is your top-tier high-performance shortboard. When the waves have shape, push, or offer any kind of section to hit, the Juliette is the board you grab. It sits above the DNA in terms of sensitivity and responsiveness --- a more refined, more reactive version built for advanced surfers who want to surf fast, tight, and on rail. Ideal for quality beachies, points, reefs, and any wave where precision matters.

The Juliette Jnr is simply a smaller version of the Stab in the Dark winning board, The Juliette (with a round tail). Proudly made in Burleigh Heads, the Juliette Jnr is complete with all of Ethan Ewing's design specifications.
Fin system: FCS II
Color: Blue, Green
Glassing: Strong Lite, 4 X 5 Oz
Rocker: DNA Rocker With Extra Nose & Tail Lift
Concave: Slight Single To Double Concave
Rails: Medium Rails
Tail: Round
Fins: 3 Fin Thruster

How it surfs: The Juliette excels in manoeuvrability, effortlessly gliding from rail to rail at high speeds with remarkable ease. It's a perfect example of a board evolving with an athlete's style and strength resulting in Ethan's own DNA model.

The MF DNA is Mick Fanning's proven high-performance shortboard for quality waves. Built for control, flow, and precision, it's the board Mick relied on during his competitive career at events like Bells, Snapper and France. It excels in bigger, punchier surf, but in the right hands it can still come alive in smaller conditions.
Fin system: FCS II / Futures
Rocker: MF Signature Rocker
Concave: Single to Double Concave
Rails: Mid to Low performance rail.
Tail: Tour Tail, Round Tail

How it surfs: Tailored around Mick's trademark 'Fanning Wrap', the MF DNA excels in 3-6ft quality conditions. It surfs with speed, drive and flow - accelerating fast, holding momentum through carves, and links sections with ease. In solid conditions, it locks in and holds it rails, giving you the confidence to push hard without losing control.
Quiver: The MF DNA is your go-to performance blade when the waves turn on.

The MF DNA is Mick Fanning's proven high-performance shortboard for quality waves. Built for control, flow, and precision, it's the board Mick relied on during his competitive career at events like Bells, Snapper and France. It excels in bigger, punchier surf, but in the right hands it can still come alive in smaller conditions.
Fin system: FCS II / Futures
Rocker: MF Signature Rocker
Concave: Single to Double Concave
Rails: Mid to low performance rail
Tail: Shortboard Performance Outline

How it surfs: Tailored around Mick's trademark 'Fanning Wrap', the MF DNA excels in 3-6ft quality conditions. It surfs with speed, drive and flow - accelerating fast, holding momentum through carves, and links sections with ease. In solid conditions, it locks in and holds it rails, giving you the confidence to push hard without losing control.
Quiver: The MF DNA is your go-to performance blade when the waves turn on.

We are thrilled to commemorate an iconic achievement in the world of surfing with the release of the Stephanie Gilmore number 8 surfboard. This exclusive range is dedicated to all surfers who aspire to ride the waves with the grace and style of the queen herself, Stephanie Gilmore.
Fin system: FCS II / Futures
Color: Purple, Orange, Clear
Glassing: Stock (4x4x4) + White Carbon Innegra Toe Patches
Rocker: Steph Signature Rocker: Low / Med / Med+
Concave: Slight Single To Double Concave
Rails: Medium / Low Rail
Tail: Narrow Round Tail
Fins: Cluster - Thruster, 3 Fin *FCS 2 or Futures

How it surfs: The SG NUMBER 8 is equipped with a slight single to double concave, providing a perfect blend of speed and control. This design ensures that you can effortlessly glide over the water while maintaining complete command over your movements. The medium low rail offers a balanced combination of stability and responsiveness, allowing you to carve through waves with precision and confidence.

Quiver: This exclusive range is dedicated to all surfers who aspire to ride the waves with the grace and style of the queen herself, Stephanie Gilmore. Designed with meticulous attention to detail, the Stephanie Gilmore number 8 surfboard draws inspiration from Steph's very own signature file. Its exceptional features make it a high-performance board for surfers who seek to excel and take their skills to the next level.

When Mick and DH were designing the MF Twin they experimented with many new ideas - outlines, rockers, concaves and fin measurements.

There were some mad scientist moments but along the way not only did they find the MF Twin but they also discovered 'UTOPIA'.
Fin system: FCS II / Futures
Color: Clear, Black, Blue
Glassing: Stock lite glassing (4x4 x 3/4 OZ)
Rocker: Flat entry rocker into a slight tail lift
Concave: Deep single into slight double concave
Rails: Medium
Tail: Squash tail
Fins: Thruster, 3 Fin *FCS 2 or Futures

How it surfs: This was the one board Mick just kept coming back to... for its speed. it was the fastest board he has ever ridden.

In research stages DH made one for Liam, who also praised it as the fastest and most stable board he has ever ridden. Liam rode 'UTOPIA' in the us open to a quarter final result. The design is getting rave reviews from all DHD team riders and is going to be a game changer!

Caution: These boards are made for high performance surfing.

Quiver: What makes this board so good is the combination of the flatter rocker and less front foot concave than the DNA.

With a wider nose and super squash tail, it glides from section to section and is controlled throughout turns. the fins are clustered together more like a twin. This board has speed and moves a lot of water. It creates less drift giving it maximum power from turn to turn.

The Phoenix -- Flight is the refined evolution of our best-selling small-wave model, built to make weak surf faster, sharper, and more dynamic. Keeping the original Phoenix DNA---easy paddle-in, instant speed, playful feel---we've reworked the outline, rocker, and concaves to push its performance further. With a sleeker nose, a narrowed tail, and a slightly thinner overall profile, the Flight version transitions quicker, turns tighter, and carries more control when the waves step up from knee-high runners to punchier, chest-high sections. Ride it as a thruster, quad, or even a twin---this board is designed to adapt to the day's conditions and your own style.
Fin system: Futures / FCS II
Color: Clear
Glassing: EPS Epoxy - 4OZ x 160G Biaxial x 4OZ with Stringer + White E-Glass Innegra Toe Patches
Rocker: Flat
Concave: Shallow Single to Double between fins
Rails: Medium Low Rails
Tail: Squash
Fins: 5 Fin - Tri Quad

How it surfs: The Phoenix -- Flight is fast, reactive, and incredibly fun. It paddles easily thanks to the fuller front end, but once you're on your feet, the refined tail and thinner profile give it the feel of a true performance shortboard. It accelerates instantly in weak surf, holds speed through slow sections, and stays lively when the waves tighten up. As a quad it becomes even faster and looser; as a thruster it adds drive and control; as a twin-plus-trailer it's playful and skatey. Whether the waves are 1ft and soft or pushing toward head high, the Flight keeps its spark, carries momentum, and gives you the confidence to push turns without bogging.

Quiver: The Phoenix - Flight is an evolved take on our best-selling small-wave model, designed to push your surfing further. It amps up responsiveness through refined design features from the original Phoenix, released in early 2020. With a sleeker, pulled-in tail and a thinner profile, this board enhances maneuverability, allowing for tighter, more controlled turns. The reduced concave under the front foot boosts speed, gliding through flat sections with ease and giving you extra drive in weaker surf. We've also added more lift and refinement to the nose outline, providing quicker transitions, smoother paddling, and sharper precision when carving. The Phoenix - Flight is built to perform in anything from playful small waves to head-high surf, giving you versatility to thrive in varying conditions. With a tri-quad fin configuration, you can ride it as a thruster, quad, or even a twin, dialling in the perfect setup for your style and the day's conditions. If you're looking for a board that's faster, more dynamic, and effortlessly sharp, the Phoenix - Flight is ready to rip into summer conditions!

Introducing the Nexus -- where innovation meets versatility.

By combining the best of our 3DV and Utopia models, our Nexus model delivers a high-performance board tailored for beach-breaks and wave pools.

The Nexus harnesses the rail-to-rail ease of the 3DV and the unparalleled speed of the Utopia, creating a dynamic shortboard that excels in smaller waves. Built specifically with wave pools in mind, it has been rigorously tested in both the ocean and several wave pools around the world, ensuring it's ready to perform in any setting.

Loved by our team riders in 1ft to 4ft waves, the Nexus offers instant acceleration and glides effortlessly through flat sections, while maintaining tight responsiveness in the pocket. Whether you're looking to push your limits in fast, critical sections or enjoy playful, smaller conditions, the Nexus delivers explosive performance, making it a must-have for those who want to dominate both the wave pool and the ocean.

Now available in our stringered EPS construction.
Fin system: Futures / FCS II
Color: Clear
Glassing: EPS Epoxy - 4OZ x 160G Biaxial x 4OZ with Stringer + White E-Glass Innegra Toe Patches
Rocker: Medium Entry, Low Exit Rocker
Concave: Single To Slight Double Concave Between Fins
Rails: Medium Rails
Tail: Slight Narrow Squash Tail
Fins: Thruster, 3 Fin.

How it surfs: Loved by our team riders in 1ft to 4ft waves, the Nexus offers instant acceleration and glides effortlessly through flat sections, while maintaining tight responsiveness in the pocket. Whether you're looking to push your limits in fast, critical sections or enjoy playful, smaller conditions, the Nexus delivers explosive performance.
Quiver: If you want explosive small-wave performance in both the ocean and wave pools, this is the board.

The Juliette is Ethan Ewing's update file that he rode to success at his historical win at Bells in 2023 and showed off its moves at Trestles during the finals, securing Ethan a solid world #2 ranking that same year. The Juliette still sits in the DNA family with more nose and tail rocker that helps with tighter turns and puts you in the pocket. Now available in our stringered EPS construction.
Fin system: Futures / FCS II
Color: Clear
Glassing: EPS Epoxy - 4OZ x 160G Biaxial x 4OZ with Stringer + White E-Glass Innegra Toe Patches
Rocker: DNA Rocker With Extra Nose & Tail Lift
Concave: Slight Single To Double Concave
Rails: Medium Rails
Tail: Slight Narrow Squash Tail
Fins: Thruster, 3 Fin *Futures or FCS II

How it surfs: The Juliette is built for speed and commitment. Reducing concave up front gives it instant acceleration --- exactly what Ethan wants when driving off the front foot. The narrowed tail lets you push hard without losing control, and the thinner, narrowed nose allows Ethan's trademark nose-pick rebounds, tight jams, and fast recoveries. The rolled deck and thinner rails make it easy to bury rail at high speed, giving that clean, powerful top-turn line Ethan is known for. It's fast, responsive, and confident when you're pushing into the lip. This board rewards surfers who drive hard, surf tight in the pocket, and want a board that keeps up with rapid direction changes.

Quiver: This is your top-tier high-performance shortboard. When the waves have shape, push, or offer any kind of section to hit, the Juliette is the board you grab. It sits above the DNA in terms of sensitivity and responsiveness --- a more refined, more reactive version built for advanced surfers who want to surf fast, tight, and on rail. Ideal for quality beachies, points, reefs, and any wave where precision matters.
Surf the 'PHOENIX' 2 to 3 inches shorter and 1 to 3 litres more than your everyday shortboard.
Fin system: FCS II / Futures
Glassing: EPS Epoxy - 4OZ x 160G Biaxial x 4OZ with Stringer + White E-Glass Innegra Toe Patches
Rocker: Flatter entry Rocker
Concave: Single to Double Concave
Rails: Medium to low rail
Tail: Swallow tail
Fins: 5 Fin *FCS 2 or Futures

How it surfs: The Phoenix is designed to make weak surf faster, sharper, and more dynamic. It offers easy paddle-in, instant speed, and a playful feel. The refined outline, rocker, and concaves push its performance further, allowing for quicker transitions, tighter turns, and more control in varying wave conditions. It can be ridden as a thruster, quad, or twin.

Quiver: The Phoenix is an evolved version of DHD's best-selling small-wave model, ideal for summer conditions and versatile enough to adapt to different wave types and your personal style. It's a board that excels in weak surf but can also handle punchier, chest-high sections.

The Black Diamond is our favourite all-rounder that can do a bit of everything. Packed with extra flotation for easy paddling and entries but still loaded with all the performance needed when the waves turn on. Now available in our stringered EPS construction.
Fin system: FCS II / Futures
Glassing: EPS Epoxy - 4OZ x 160G Biaxial x 4OZ with Stringer + White E-Glass Innegra Toe Patches
Rocker: Medium to Low
Concave: Single to Double Concave to Vee
Rails: Thinned out rails
Tail: Rounded Pin
Fins: Quad, 5 Fin *FCS 2 or Futures

How it surfs: The Black Diamond is a versatile all-rounder, offering extra flotation for easy paddling and wave entry. It provides performance for steeper, better waves while remaining forgiving in small, weak surf. The timber stringer and BIAX cloth construction make it stronger, more responsive, and suitable for all conditions, reducing chatter and improving control. The thinned-out rails help the board bite and engage more easily for rail-to-rail turns.

Quiver: The Black Diamond is a genuine one-board quiver designed to handle almost anything you're likely to surf. It's a board that covers a lot of ground and suits a wide range of surfers and conditions. It can be ridden as a Twin, Thruster, Two plus One, or a Quad, offering endless opportunities for enjoyment.

The Juliette Jnr EPS is simply a smaller version of the Stab in the Dark winning board, The Juliette, in our Stringered EPS Construction.

The Juliette is Ethan Ewing's update file that he rode to success at his historical win at Bells in 2023 and showed off its moves at Trestles during the finals, securing Ethan a solid world #2 ranking that same year. The Juliette still sits in the DNA family with more nose and tail rocker that helps with tighter turns and puts you in the pocket. Now available in our stringered EPS construction.

Rocker: DNA Rocker With Extra Nose & Tail Lift
Concave: Slight Single To Double Concave
Rails: Medium Rails
Tail: Slight Narrow Squash Tail
Fins: Thruster, 3 Fin *Futures or FCS II

The narrowed tail lets you push hard without losing control, and the thinner, narrowed nose allows Ethan's trademark nose-pick rebounds, tight jams, and fast recoveries. The rolled deck and thinner rails make it easy to bury rail at high speed, giving that clean, powerful top-turn line Ethan is known for. It's fast, responsive, and confident when you're pushing into the lip. This board rewards surfers who drive hard, surf tight in the pocket, and want a board that keeps up with rapid direction changes.

It sits above the DNA in terms of sensitivity and responsiveness --- a more refined, more reactive version built for advanced surfers who want to surf fast, tight, and on rail. Ideal for quality beachies, points, reefs, and any wave where precision matters.

The best performing travel board... the ultimate step up!

So what's different from the 3.0?

The nose is a touch narrower with more lift, creating less catch up-front when you're tight in the pocket. A rolled deck provides extra volume in the centre, giving you a lower & more refined rail. The tail has been pulled in, so you can push harder through turns with more manoeuvrability.

The 4.0 paddles better, goes faster & can be pushed harder in heavier conditions, when you need it the most!
Fin system: FCS II / Futures
Glassing: 6 x 4x4 *Glassed Strong
Rocker: More Nose & Tail Rocker
Concave: Slight Single to Longer Double Concave
Rails: More Roll in Deck for Lower Rail
Tail: Rounded Pin Tail
Fins: 5 Fin *FCS 2 or Futures
How it surfs: The 4.0 paddles better, goes faster & can be pushed harder in heavier conditions, when you need it the most!
Quiver: The ultimate travel board for pumping waves.

The Black Diamond is DHD's true all-rounder --- a genuine one-board quiver designed to handle almost anything you're likely to surf. With extra flotation through the front half for easy paddling and wave entry, it stays forgiving and user-friendly in small, weak surf, while still offering enough performance to handle steeper, better waves when they turn on. It's a board that covers a lot of ground and suits a wide range of surfers and conditions.
Fin system: FCS II / Futures
Glassing: Standard PU glassing (4 × 4 × 4), also available in EPS Epoxy
Rocker: Medium to low entry rocker for speed and easy acceleration
Concave: Single to double concave flowing into vee out the tail
Rails: Forgiving through the front, more refined through the tai
Tail: Rounded pin tail
Fins: Five-fin setup (Thruster or Quad compatible -- FCS II or Futures)

How it surfs: The Black Diamond is easy to paddle, quick to get moving, and forgiving underfoot. The extra width and volume up front help generate speed early and glide through weaker sections, while the pulled-in rounded pin tail and refined rear rails let the board sit comfortably in the pocket when waves get steeper. It feels fast off the front foot, smooth through turns, and predictable at speed. Whether it's one-foot slop or cleaner, more powerful surf, the Black Diamond adapts without feeling out of place.

Quiver: This is your do-everything board --- the one you grab when you don't want to think too hard about conditions. If you want a single board that paddles easily, works across a wide wave range, and still lets you surf properly when the waves improve, the Black Diamond earns its spot.
The DHD Interceptor is Darren's version of a mid length, it's designed for the surfer who wants an easy paddle, smooth line's and tighter turns for avoiding traffic in the lineup.
Fin system: FCS II / Futures
Color: White, Aqua, Sky
Glassing: Strong ( 6 x 4 x 4)
Rocker: Flat entry rocker to slight tail lift
Concave: Entry vee to slight concave into double vee out the tail
Tail: Rounded Pin with Reinforced tail patches
Fins: Quad, 5 Fin *FCS 2 or Futures
How it surfs: The DHD Interceptor is Darren's version of a mid length, it's designed for the surfer who wants an easy paddle, smooth line's and tighter turns for avoiding traffic in the lineup.

Our goal for the sandman project was to develop a board that would make surfing easier for the 'normal' person, but also have some versatility to be used as a semi step-up board for the more regular surfer.
Fin system: FCS II / Futures
Glassing: Standard ( 4 x 4 x 4)
Rocker: Flat entry rocker into a slight tail lift.
Concave: Slight Single into slight Double Concave
Rails: Sleek medium low rail
Tail: Round tail
Fins: Thruster, 3 Fin *FCS 2 or Futures

How it surfs: Darren designed the Sandman with the wide point moved forward 2 inches and the deck rolled into a sleek medium low rail. This design provides easy paddling without losing the signature DHD performance. The rounded pin tail allows smooth rail to rail surfing, the slight single to double concave with a flat entry rocker generates the speed that you are looking for.

The MF Twin developed with more drive, more speed for more conditions.

The MF twin with a horseshoe tail has more rocker, more concave and a straighter tail outline than a traditional twin fin. The design specification deliver Mick's trademark speed, tighter turns in the pocket and more hold through carving turns.
Fin system: FCS II / FUTURES
Color: Teal, Bronze, Graphite, White
Glassing: Deck 4x4oz / Bottom 4oz
Rocker: Medium to Full Rocker with deep single concave for extra speed.

Concave: Slight single entry concave to deep single with slight double concave into flat on the last 3 inches of tail. Concave's are used to channel the direction of the water flow under the surfboard towards the fins. They also allow air to get under the surfboard when you are surfing and will increase both its sensitivity and speed.
Rails: Rolled Deck to Medium Low Rail
Tail: Horseshoe tail shape
Fins: Twin Fin with FCS II or Futures

How it surfs: The MF Twin with a horseshoe tail has more rocker, more concave and a straighter tail outline than a traditional twin fin. The design specifications deliver Mick's trademark speed, tighter turns in the pocket and more hold through carving turns.
The Twin Fin has been Designed for Summer Fun! A little nostalgia with a modern day twist makes this perfect for 1 to 4ft surf.

Rocker: Medium to Low
Concave: Single to Double with Vee
Tail: Twin Fin *Futures or FCS 2

Perfect for 1 to 4ft surf the Mini Twin is a twin fin surfboard that's a little shorter and a little rounder than its big brother the DHD Twin Fin. This retro-inspired surfboard comes with reinforced carbon in the fin boxes and tail that will give you plenty of control and strength through your back foot.

Surf the 'Phoenix' 2 to 3 inches shorter and 1 to 3 litres more than your everyday shortboard.

Rails: Medium to low rail

How it surfs: The Phoenix is designed to be surfed 2 to 3 inches shorter and with 1 to 3 litres more volume than your everyday shortboard. It's built to make weak surf faster, sharper, and more dynamic, offering easy paddle-in, instant speed, and a playful feel. The Flight version, with its sleeker nose, narrowed tail, and slightly thinner profile, transitions quicker, turns tighter, and provides more control in punchier, chest-high waves. It can be ridden as a thruster, quad, or twin, adapting to various conditions and surfing styles.

Quiver: The Phoenix is your go-to board for small to medium waves, perfect for adding spark and release to your surfing. It's ideal for everyday surfing in average wave conditions from 1 to 4ft, and the Flight version can handle up to head-high waves. It's a versatile addition to your quiver, allowing you to adapt your fin setup (thruster, quad, or twin) to match the day's conditions and your personal style.

Fin system: FCS II / Futures
Glassing: Our stock glassing consists of 1 x 4oz layer on the bottom and 2 x 4oz fiberglass on the deck of the surfboard - 4 x 4x4.
